tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild renal impairment (elevated creatinine), significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, with low blood glucose and HbA1c, suggesting possible acute metabolic disturbance (e.g., hypoglycemia, possible diabetic or non-diabetic ketoacidosis). Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate dehydration or underlying renal/urinary pathology. Recommend urgent clinical assessment for metabolic and renal status, and to rule out infection or acute kidney injury.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
